# Gmsh medium: 3D mesh generation

Wrap the `gmsh` CLI, feed it a tiny `.geo` model, and verify a 3D mesh file is produced.

## Why This Case Exists

A medium Gmsh challenge should move beyond the shallowest 2D path and prove a real volume-meshing workflow through the generated CLI wrapper.

## Case Shape

- ID: `gmsh_cli`
- Family: `gmsh`
- Difficulty: `medium`
- Tier: `gha_subset`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `gmsh`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/gmsh_cli/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

This case relies on its workflow without extra breadth assertions.

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=gmsh_cli
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only gmsh_cli
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/gmsh_cli/fixtures/box.geo`

## Workflow

### 1. Generate a 3D mesh from the tiny Gmsh fixture

Tool: `run_gmsh`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "extra_argv": [
    "{challenge_fixture_dir}/box.geo",
    "-3",
    "-o",
    "{challenge_artifact_dir}/box3d.msh"
  ]
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['{challenge_artifact_dir}/box3d.msh'].

## What This Case Proves

- A standalone engineering CLI can be scaffolded from live help output.
- The generated wrapper can pass through real command-line arguments deterministically.
- A raw upstream command can emit a non-empty engineering artifact under `mcpwrap` control.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: treat a meshing command like a real external engineering executable.
# The checked-in `.geo` file keeps the case tiny while still producing a real mesh artifact.
id = "gmsh_cli"
title = "Gmsh medium: 3D mesh generation"
tier = "gha_subset"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"
family = "gmsh"
difficulty = "medium"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `gmsh` CLI, feed it a tiny `.geo` model, and verify a 3D mesh file is produced."
motivation = "A medium Gmsh challenge should move beyond the shallowest 2D path and prove a real volume-meshing workflow through the generated CLI wrapper."
proves = [
  "A standalone engineering CLI can be scaffolded from live help output.",
  "The generated wrapper can pass through real command-line arguments deterministically.",
  "A raw upstream command can emit a non-empty engineering artifact under `mcpwrap` control.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["gmsh"]

[probe]
commands = [["gmsh"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_gmsh"
help_probe_args = ["-help"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Generate a 3D mesh from the tiny Gmsh fixture"
tool = "run_gmsh"
arguments = { extra_argv = ["{challenge_fixture_dir}/box.geo", "-3", "-o", "{challenge_artifact_dir}/box3d.msh"] }

[workflow.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = ["{challenge_artifact_dir}/box3d.msh"]
```
